text.title: Java Spring Boot Enterprise Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real enterprise Spring Boot 3 codebases. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Adding an entity to `HashSet` triggers infinite recursion; `equals` walks lazy collections and loads them.
antipattern.root-cause: Author put `@Data` on a JPA entity; generated `equals` / `hashCode` traverse all fields.
antipattern.fix: Use `@Getter @Setter` only; implement `equals`/`hashCode` over a business key on top of `BaseEntity.id`.

antipattern.symptom: List endpoint executes 1 + N queries per row; profile shows N additional `SELECT` per page.
antipattern.root-cause: Mapper or controller accesses a lazy association after the transaction closes.
antipattern.fix: Project to DTO inside the service `@Transactional` boundary; use `@EntityGraph` / `JOIN FETCH`. Add `n-plus-one-assertion.java` test that asserts a max query count.

antipattern.symptom: Concurrent edits silently overwrite each other; audit trail shows lost updates with no exception.
antipattern.root-cause: Entity did not extend `BaseEntity`; `@Version` field missing; last-write-wins.
antipattern.fix: Inherit `BaseEntity`; catch `OptimisticLockException` in the service and surface 409 via `ProblemDetail`.

antipattern.symptom: Each controller returns a different JSON error shape; clients implement N different parsers.
antipattern.root-cause: No `@RestControllerAdvice`; each controller invents its own.
antipattern.fix: Add one `@RestControllerAdvice` mapping every domain exception → `ProblemDetail`. Delete per-controller `try/catch` for cross-cutting handling.

antipattern.symptom: Read-heavy endpoint runs slow under load; Hibernate flushes the dirty-check cache on every method exit.
antipattern.root-cause: Service has no class-level `@Transactional(readOnly = true)`; every method opens a writable transaction.
antipattern.fix: Add `@Transactional(readOnly = true)` at class level; override on write methods. Verify with a single integration test that read endpoints log zero `UPDATE` statements.
